summary.intent: Fix silent truncation of AGENTS.md and TOOLS.md at 6,000 characters by making the limit configurable, raising the default, adding logging on truncation, and surfacing truncation metadata in the full_context API response.
summary.outcome: All five files were modified, PR #610 created and iterated on to address Greptile review comments. A merge conflict with upstream (which independently implemented a more comprehensive version of the same fix) was resolved by adopting upstream's architecture and adding the branch's unique contribution: tracing::warn! on truncation.

repo: WORKSPACE_FILE_MAX_CHARS was hardcoded at 6,000 in crates/agents/src/prompt.rs with no logging or config override.
Config schema changes require updates in three places: schema.rs (struct field + default fn), validate.rs (build_schema_map Leaf entry), and template.rs (commented example line).
The project uses tracing crate directly (not feature-gated) in moltis-agents for structured logging.
Semantic validation warnings for bad config values live in check_semantic_warnings() in crates/config/src/validate.rs.

Upstream independently implemented similar changes (with 32K default vs our 50K) using richer types: PromptBuildLimits, PromptBuildOutput, PromptBuildMetadata, WorkspaceFilePromptStatus, truncate_prompt_text_details.
just lint / clippy fails on llama-cpp-sys-2 due to missing cmake/CUDA — this is a pre-existing environment issue unrelated to Rust code changes.
The project uses beads CLI for issue tracking alongside GitHub PRs.

code.finding: Original WORKSPACE_FILE_MAX_CHARS = 6_000 hardcoded constant, now replaced by configurable limit via PromptBuildLimits.

append_truncated_text_block and truncate_prompt_text — refactored to return (String, bool) tuple to avoid double O(n) chars().count() traversal; also fixed edge case where max_chars==0 with non-empty text incorrectly returned was_truncated=false.
build_schema_map requires explicit Leaf entries for all config fields under their parent struct key.
full_context() response extended with workspaceFiles array containing per-file truncation metadata (file, chars, limit, truncated).
After merge, had duplicate workspace_file_max_chars lines (50000 from branch, 32000 from upstream) — needed manual deduplication.

workflow: Running cargo clippy on specific crates (--package flag) avoids noise from unrelated build failures in the workspace.

When upstream independently solves the same problem with a richer architecture, the right merge strategy is to take upstream's types/structure and port only the unique additions (tracing::warn! in this case).
git checkout --theirs on conflicted files followed by manually re-adding unique contributions is cleaner than attempting three-way conflict resolution for large structural refactors.
Greptile bot posts iterative review summaries as issue comments rather than inline PR review comments — check both gh pr view --comments and gh api for review comments.
The pattern unwrap_or_else(|| ChatConfig::default().workspace_file_max_chars) is preferred over repeating the magic constant as a fallback.

friction: Upstream independently implemented the same feature with different architecture (32K default, richer types), causing a three-way merge conflict across all five modified files.
Template.rs auto-merged with duplicate workspace_file_max_chars lines (both 50000 and 32000) requiring manual cleanup.
just lint / clippy fails workspace-wide due to llama-cpp-sys-2 requiring cmake/CUDA, forcing workaround of running clippy per-package.
truncate_prompt_text returning a plain String required refactoring to (String, bool) tuple to avoid double chars().count() traversal — this was a non-obvious perf issue caught in review.
Greptile review comments required three rounds of fixes: (1) duplicated magic constant, (2) double traversal + zero-value guard, (3) max_chars==0 truncation flag edge case.
open_items: Web UI indicator on Settings > Agent page showing AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md size and truncation status — requires JS changes in page-agents.js and new RPC endpoint or gon data.
Dynamic scaling of workspace_file_max_chars based on model context window — requires model resolution before prompt construction, changing call order.
Upstream chose 32K as the default; branch used 50K — the merged result uses 32K (upstream wins), but the rationale for the difference was never reconciled.
